rationale: Dim1: Control case; output p=33 falls in 30<p<50 band -> 1 (would need p<=30 for 2). Dim2/Dim3: control -> null per rubric; output correctly lists no mechanism hypotheses. Dim4: Consistency rule satisfied (p=33 <40 with tier 'watch', allowed) so no cap. Evidence is specific and multi-point: exact goodwill/intangibles jump Q3->Q4 2010 (217,204,000->355,842,000; 37,356,000->72,269,000) against flat quarterly revenue (1,316,948,000 vs 1,313,426,000), correctly interpreted as a discrete acquisition; multi-year NI vs OCF comparison (2009: 61,945,000 vs 984,956,000; 2013: 237,792,000 vs 1,223,082,000) correctly read as a leasing-business quality positive; full 2011 quarterly NI/revenue seasonality path; allowance (13,808,000->16,955,000) vs receivables (598,661,000->777,370,000) growth 2009-2013; concrete 10-K/A chronology (2012-02-16 original vs 2012-03-19 amendment) hedged as possibly administrative. Coherent multi-year combination supports the low-probability call -> 3. Minor blemish: one CL7 evidence item ('NT 11-K filed 1996-07-01', source 'n/a') is unverifiable, but it is a marginal aside and does not dominate the citation base. Memorization condition2: reasoning anchors on concrete provided data (not bare document lists) and mentions no post-cutoff revelation/enforcement/outcome facts -> false.
